title: OSMM Customer Insight Object
description: Learning-category object: a durable, interpreted finding about customer behavior or response (Phase 7 sub-process 7.2), drawn from one or more Performance Measurements and external evidence. Where a Performance Measurement is the number, a Customer Insight is the why. It closes the loop by proposing updates back into the Context layer (sub-process 7.7) via lean pointer-plus-prose, so applying an insight produces a new version of the target object. Canonical schema for object_type 'customer_insight'. The osmm-customer-insight-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

customer_insight_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'CIN-' + lowercase hyphen slug describing the finding (e.g. 'CIN-wendys-checkout-fee-friction'). Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

name.description: Short readable label for the insight (e.g. "Deal-savvy cravers abandon at the payment step").

insight_statement.description: The finding itself, stated as an interpreted conclusion — not a metric. The 'why' behind the numbers (e.g. "Deal-savvy cravers abandon at the payment step because of surprise fees, not price sensitivity").

confidence.description: How well-supported the insight is by its evidence. Optional.

evidence.description: What the insight is drawn from. Optional.

linked_performance_measurements.description: Performance Measurements that evidence this insight.

external_sources.description: Non-OSMM evidence (research, qualitative studies, support logs) cited for the insight.

affected_personas.description: Personas the insight is about. Use 'PER-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until built. Optional.

affected_audiences.description: Audiences the insight is about. Use 'AUD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until built. Optional.

segment_note.description: Free-text scope when the affected group is not yet a modeled Persona or Audience. Optional.

implication.description: The 'so what' — why this matters for the marketing decisions downstream. Optional.

proposes_updates_to.description: The Context-layer write-back (sub-process 7.7): durable changes this insight proposes to Business Context, Brand Context, Product Context, Persona, or Marketing Strategy. A lean pointer plus prose — applying a change produces a new version of the target object; this object records the proposal, it does not patch the target. Optional.

target_id.description: Id of the object to update (e.g. a Persona PER-, Brand Context BRC-, Marketing Strategy MKS-).

recommended_change.description: Prose description of the proposed change to that object.

linked_business_context.description: Id of the owning Business Context. Use 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date.
